contentBlocks.original:
मूषिकवाहन मोदकहस्त
चामरकर्ण विलम्बितसूत्र ।
वामनरूप महेस्वरपुत्र
विघ्नविनायक पाद नमस्ते ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mūṣikavāhana modakahasta
cāmarakarṇa vilambitasūtra .
vāmanarūpa mahesvaraputra
vighnavināyaka pāda namaste ..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Sri Vighna Vinayaka, whose vehicle is the mouse and who has the modaka in his hand, whose large ears are like fans and who wears a long sacred thread, who is short in stature and is the son of Sri Maheswara (Lord Shiva), prostrations at the feet of Sri Vighna Vinayaka, the remover of the obstacles of his devotees.
contentBlocks.meanings: मूषिकवाहन (mūṣikavāhana) – mouse vehicle (mūṣika:mouse | vāhana:vehicle); मोदकहस्त (modakahasta) – modaka in hand (modaka:sweet dumpling | hasta:hand); चामरकर्ण (cāmarakarṇa) – fan-like ears (cāmara:fan | karṇa:ear); विलम्बितसूत्र (vilambitasūtra) – wearing long thread (vilambita:hanging | sūtra:thread); वामनरूप (vāmanarūpa) – short stature (vāmana:dwarf | rūpa:form); महेस्वरपुत्र (mahesvaraputra) – son of Maheswara (mahesvara:Lord Shiva | putra:son); विघ्नविनायक (vighnavināyaka) – remover of obstacles (vighna:obstacle | vināyaka:remover); पाद (pāda) – feet; नमस्ते (namaste) – salutations;
